title: arunner plan
description: Input to `tick.py --init`. One run per entry. dispatch_mode is 'subagent' (in-session, cadence rung 1) or 'shell' (detached host-CLI, cadence rungs 2-4 / the ticker). Top-level knobs default in the tick script if omitted.

tick_interval_minutes.description: Minutes the orchestrator waits between ticks (ScheduleWakeup cadence) while work is in flight.

pool_size.description: Max concurrent in-flight runs (claimed/running/stalled). Subscription concurrency caps may force a lower value.

stall_threshold_minutes.description: A run whose heartbeat mtime is older than this is marked stalled. The mandatory ~3-min worker keepalive keeps a live run well under it.

launch_grace_minutes.description: A claimed run that emits NO heartbeat within this window is marked auth_or_launch_failed (the dispatch never started a worker).

idle_tick_multiplier.description: Lengthens the tick cadence by this factor when nothing is actively running (all waiting/stalled). 1 = uniform cadence.

vars.description: FR-61 (optional): plan-level vars map merged under each entry/step vars for the {key} replace-key pass. Same constraints as entry-level vars (no reserved tokens as values; reserved names not valid keys).

allow_reasoning_gates.description: FR-63 (optional, default false): opt-in required before any reasoning gate (kind:'reasoning') is permitted. Even when true, a reasoning gate is rejected at --check inside a measurement:true run, an FR-51 harness run, or an FR-55-audited run.

measurement.description: FR-63 (optional, default false): marks a measurement/benchmark run. When true, only deterministic shell gates are allowed — any reasoning gate is rejected at --check (the one optional AI decision is fenced out of measured runs, NFR-12).

items.description: FR-61 constraint relaxation: worker_prompt is no longer unconditionally required. Exactly one prompt-source must be present per entry — inline worker_prompt, worker_prompt_file (FR-61), steps (FR-62), or adapter (FR-40/41) — enforced via oneOf, mirroring heartbeat.schema.json's oneOf. The stdlib `--check` validator (FR-42, NFR-3) is the runtime enforcer; this oneOf documents the contract.

task_id.description: Stable id for this run, threaded into the heartbeat and result. A2A-ready: a UUID is recommended.

target_repo.description: Absolute path the worker operates on. Council A-2: absolute, never cwd-derived.

dispatch_mode.description: v1.5.9: 'subagent' (in-session agent Task/Agent tool, cadence rung 1 only) or 'shell' (detached host-CLI subprocess; REQUIRED for cadence rungs 2-4 / the ticker). schema_version stays '1' — 'shell' is an additive enum value.

worker_prompt.description: The worker prompt. MUST contain the {HEARTBEAT_PATH}/{TASK_ID}/{RUN_DIR}/{TARGET_REPO} placeholders (plus {HARNESS_BIN} for the helper/demo-worker location) the tick engine substitutes with absolute paths at dispatch time (Council A-2 / FR-21a — no model-transcribed paths). subagent mode: passed verbatim to the subagent. shell mode: written to queue/job-NNNNN.prompt.txt (quoting/arg-length safety) and referenced by worker_cmd via {PROMPT_FILE}.

worker_prompt_file.description: FR-61 (optional; mutually exclusive with worker_prompt — both = --check error). Path to a Markdown/text prompt file used instead of inline worker_prompt. Resolved relative to the PLAN FILE's directory (absolute taken as-is) and SNAPSHOTTED into the run-dir at --init (NFR-9 self-sufficient record) — mutating the source afterward never changes the dispatched prompt. The same {HEARTBEAT_PATH}/{TASK_ID}/{RUN_DIR}/{TARGET_REPO}/{HARNESS_BIN} reserved placeholders are substituted at dispatch (FR-21a), after the vars replace-key pass.

vars.description: FR-61 (optional): a flat operator-declared map substituted into worker_prompt/worker_prompt_file by a designated replace-key pass (literal str.replace of each {key}), run BEFORE the engine's reserved placeholders. Only declared keys are replaced; stray braces are NOT scanned or rejected, so literal single-brace JSON blocks survive unmangled. A vars value may not contain a reserved engine token and a reserved placeholder name is not a valid vars key (both --check errors). May also be set per-step (FR-62) and merged.

steps.description: FR-62 (optional; mutually exclusive with a top-level prompt-source — both = --check error). Ordered sub-runs executed SEQUENTIALLY within the entry, occupying ONE pool slot for the whole sequence. Each step is a full FR-18 sub-run on disk at run-NN/steps/step-MM/ (own manifest.json/heartbeat.ndjson/result, reusing job_manifest.schema.json with dispatch_mode widened to include 'shell'). The engine dispatches only the current step per tick; resume reads step_index and reaps-not-re-runs completed steps (NFR-6).

items.description: One step. Carries its own prompt-source (worker_prompt or worker_prompt_file) plus optional per-step target_repo/worker_cmd/adapter/vars and an optional gate (FR-63) evaluated before the NEXT step dispatches.

label.description: Short display label shown as 'step N of M' in the status table/monitor (FR-27/59).

worker_prompt.description: Inline step prompt (mutually exclusive with worker_prompt_file).

worker_prompt_file.description: FR-61 prompt-from-file for this step (mutually exclusive with worker_prompt).

target_repo.description: Optional per-step override; defaults to the entry's target_repo.

vars.description: Optional per-step vars, merged over the entry/plan vars.

gate.description: FR-63 (optional): a continuation gate evaluated after THIS step reaps clean and before the next step dispatches. Recorded as step-MM/gate.json and read-on-resume (never recomputed, NFR-6). Resolves to a FR-64 closed-set outcome.

kind.description: 'shell' (DEFAULT, only kind allowed in measurement runs): operator argv, exit code -> FR-64 outcome, EXIT-CODE ONLY (no stdout/regex interpretation — avoids the engine-parses-text hazard FR-40/41/56). 'reasoning' (OPTIONAL, FENCED): requires plan-level allow_reasoning_gates:true; separate judging context (same-context judge = --check error, FR-51); structured closed-set verdict via FR-18 data; malformed/absent -> internal_error fail-closed; rejected at --check in any measurement/FR-51/FR-55-audited run.

argv.description: shell gate: the argv to run (placeholders and vars substituted by the engine). Its exit code maps to the FR-64 outcome.

skip_to.description: Optional target for a 'skip-to-next' outcome: skip forward to this named step (skipped steps get a synthesized auditable 'skipped' terminal).

heartbeat_path.description: FR-20 (optional): an ABSOLUTE path to a heartbeat file the job already writes. When set, the harness watches THIS file instead of assigning the run-dir default {HEARTBEAT_PATH} (and {HEARTBEAT_PATH} resolves to it for the worker) — so a pre-existing tool with its own status-file location needs no change. The run-dir result/manifest layout is unaffected.

worker_cmd.description: shell mode only: argv template the ticker Popens detached. Tokens may carry the placeholder block PLUS {PROMPT_FILE} (the per-job prompt file). Example: ["claude","--print","--model","sonnet","--append-system-prompt","$(cat {PROMPT_FILE})"] or ["python3","<abs>/harness_demo_worker.py","--heartbeat-path","{HEARTBEAT_PATH}","--task-id","{TASK_ID}","--run-dir","{RUN_DIR}"].

auth_check.description: shell mode only (optional): a cheap argv the ticker runs once before the first shell dispatch to verify the host CLI is present + authenticated (FR-16). A nonzero result marks the entry AUTH_OR_LAUNCH_FAILED with an actionable message instead of silently stalling.

adapter.description: FR-41 one-field selector. When set, the engine SYNTHESIZES the shell worker_cmd to invoke heartbeat.py, so the operator declares intent in one place and never hand-wires the helper. 'wrap': run `command` as a child and emit its heartbeat stream (doneness = exit code). 'tail': watch `log_path` a job already writes (doneness precedence: process-exit authoritative; optional success_regex/failure_regex/sentinel_file overlay; default COMPLETED on a clean exit). An adapter entry runs as dispatch_mode 'shell' and does not require worker_prompt/worker_cmd.

command.description: adapter only: the argv to run. Required for adapter 'wrap'; optional for 'tail' (when arunner launches+owns the job that writes log_path).

log_path.description: adapter 'tail' only (required): the log file the job writes; its most recent line becomes the IN_PROGRESS label.

success_regex.description: adapter 'tail' (optional overlay): a log line matching this maps to COMPLETED.

failure_regex.description: adapter 'tail' (optional overlay): a log line matching this maps to FAILED (failure wins over success on a line).

sentinel_file.description: adapter 'tail' (optional overlay): this file existing maps to COMPLETED.

pid.description: adapter 'tail' (optional): watch an external process's exit as the authoritative doneness signal (recorded in the claim lock for the engine's dead-PID backstop).
